findings.evidence: `EvenPrimeDiscriminant.lean:45-47` lists the even discriminants and `EvenPrimeDiscriminant.lean:71-80` gives radicands `-1`, `2`, `-2`; the current signature at lines 75-78 excludes all even-even pairs.
findings.fix: Weaken `hnot` to exclude just `(D,E) = (8,-8)` and `(-8,8)` (or equivalently the distinct even pair whose radicand natAbs are both `2`).
findings.issue: `isCoprime_primeDiscriminantRadicand` assumes the two discriminants are not both even, but `-4` with either `8` or `-8` has radicands `-1` with `2` or `-2`, hence is coprime; only the pair `8`,`-8` is non-coprime among distinct even prime discriminants.

findings.evidence: `EvenPrimeDiscriminant.lean:71-80` shows the three even radicands are `-1`, `2`, `-2`; only the product of all three is the square `4`, while the line 105-109 theorem excludes valid two-even cases.
findings.fix: State the natural prime-discriminant independence criterion by forbidding the simultaneous presence of all three even discriminants `-4`, `8`, and `-8`, and derive the current at-most-one-even genus-field specialization as a corollary if needed.
findings.issue: `not_isSquare_prod_primeDiscriminantRadicands` requires at most one even member, but injective families with exactly two even members are still square-class independent; e.g. `[-4,8]` has subset products `-1`, `2`, and `-2`, none a rational square.

summary: The new prime-discriminant APIs are under-generalized in the even cases. They exclude valid independent or coprime families that are already visible from the existing even-prime-discriminant classification.
